name: Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries
climate: Predominantly arid and tropical; desert in Middle East and North Africa, tropical in West and Central Africa, tropical/arid in Venezuela

neighbors.name: OPEC+
neighbors.relevance: Extended alliance adding Russia, Kazakhstan, and others for broader production coordination

neighbors.name: Gulf Cooperation Council
neighbors.relevance: SAU, ARE, KWT are both OPEC and GCC members; Gulf states hold most spare capacity

neighbors.name: Persian Gulf
neighbors.relevance: Five OPEC members border the Gulf; largest concentration of OPEC production

neighbors.name: Strait of Hormuz
neighbors.relevance: Critical export chokepoint for Gulf-based OPEC members

neighbors.name: ECOWAS
neighbors.relevance: Nigeria is both an OPEC and ECOWAS member; Gulf of Guinea oil production

links.description: OPEC+ extends coordination to non-OPEC producers for greater market control
SAU, ARE, KWT are core members of both OPEC and GCC; Saudi Arabia leads both
Gulf OPEC members depend on Hormuz for seaborne oil exports
G20 nations are the primary consumers of OPEC oil; price negotiations affect global economy
SAU, ARE, IRN joined BRICS; aligning OPEC interests with emerging economy bloc
China is the largest single buyer of OPEC crude; BRI investments flow to OPEC members
OPEC oil bound for Europe transits the Red Sea and Suez Canal
Seven OPEC members are African states; AU energy policy intersects with OPEC quotas
